back: A fast, open-source engine for processing big data across a cluster, born at UC Berkeley in 2009.
Keeping data in RAM between steps instead of writing to disk each time — Spark's key speed advantage over MapReduce.
A group of machines working together as one. In Spark, made of a driver plus executors.
The 'brain' of a Spark cluster: it plans the job and splits it into tasks for the workers.
The worker processes in a Spark cluster, each crunching a chunk of the data in parallel.
Spark's friendly abstraction: a distributed table of rows and columns you shape with simple commands.
Transformations (filter, join) build a plan; an action (count, save) actually triggers execution.
Spark delays running work until an action is called, so it can see the whole plan and optimize it first.
You can drive Spark with Python, SQL, Scala, or R instead of low-level Java.
One Spark engine handles batch ETL, SQL, streaming, and machine learning — no separate tools required.
